intent: Implement GDPR Article 17 registry-driven erasure cascade — hardDeleteUser helper, cancel-account-deletion, retention-tick skeleton, registry-drift probe tests

findings.title: retention-tick has no authentication — anyone can trigger hard-deletes
findings.suggested_fix: Add a shared-secret header check: require an `X-Retention-Secret` header matching a `RETENTION_TICK_SECRET` env var. Reject with 401 if missing/wrong.
findings.why_it_matters: Any external party who discovers the endpoint URL can POST to it and trigger the retention-tick loop, hard-deleting all accounts whose grace period has expired. While the function only operates on legitimately expired accounts, unauthenticated triggering is a security concern.
evidence: retention-tick/index.ts:60-62: accepts POST and GET with no auth check — no JWT, no shared secret, no IP allowlist

findings.title: retention-tick fallback for missing pending_deletion_at not implemented
findings.suggested_fix: Either: (a) make the metadata stamp failure fatal (return 500 instead of logging and continuing), or (b) add a fallback in retention-tick to also catch users with deleted_at older than 7+N days but no pending_deletion_at in metadata. Option (a) is simpler.
findings.why_it_matters: delete-account comment says 'retention-tick will fall back to checking deleted_at' but retention-tick only checks raw_user_meta_data.pending_deletion_at. If updateUserById fails silently, the user's data will never be hard-deleted — a GDPR erasure gap.
evidence: delete-account/index.ts:123-130: metaError is non-blocking; comment promises fallback
retention-tick/index.ts:1265-1274: only checks meta?.pending_deletion_at — no deleted_at fallback

findings.title: Stripe metadata key assumption undocumented — silent anonymisation bypass
findings.suggested_fix: Add a code comment documenting that Stripe customers must be created with metadata key `supabase_uid` set to the Supabase user UUID. Also check stripe-webhook/index.ts and create-checkout/index.ts to verify this metadata is indeed set on customer creation.
findings.why_it_matters: If Stripe customers were created without the `supabase_uid` metadata key, the search returns no customers and stripeAnonymised is set to true (success) — but no actual anonymisation occurred. The operator would believe PII was scrubbed when it wasn't.
evidence: hardDeleteUser.ts:186-189: if customers.length === 0 → stripeAnonymised = true (silent success)

findings.title: HARD_DELETE_TABLE_NAMES duplicated in 3 places — maintenance burden
findings.suggested_fix: Add a prominent comment at the top of the HARD_DELETE_TABLE_NAMES declaration in deleteAccount.test.ts explaining all 3 locations that must be updated in sync: (1) this file, (2) _shared/hardDeleteUser.ts TABLE_NAMES, (3) tableRegistry.ts ERASURE_TABLE_NAMES. The drift test will catch number mismatches but not ordering differences.
findings.why_it_matters: Three separate copies of the same table list (test file inline, hardDeleteUser.ts TABLE_NAMES, tableRegistry.ts ERASURE_TABLE_NAMES) means three manual edits per new table. The drift test catches count divergence but ordering differences in TABLE_NAMES vs ERASURE_TABLE_NAMES would be invisible.

residual_risks: retention-tick is unauthenticated — see P1 finding. Until secured, it should not be exposed publicly.

Storage removal only handles flat-prefix objects (userId/filename). Nested paths (userId/subfolder/filename) would not be included in the list prefix — verify actual Storage path structure matches the prefix assumption.

The cascade processes tables serially, which for 38 tables could take 10-30 seconds per user. If the Edge Function times out (60s default), some tables may not be deleted. Consider parallelizing table deletes with Promise.allSettled.
testing_gaps: No test verifies that retention-tick correctly filters users whose pending_deletion_at is within the grace period (not yet expired) — only expired users should be deleted.
No integration test for the two-call sequence in cancel-account-deletion (clear metadata then reactivate) — specifically the failure case where first call succeeds but second fails.
The probe tests assert table names match but don't exercise the actual hardDeleteUser logic — acceptable for the Deno/Vitest boundary reason documented in the test, but worth noting.
